steps.title: Match the coefficient condition
steps.content: A linear equation in one variable has no solution when the $x$-coefficients match but the constants are different. Match the $x$-coefficients first.

steps.title: Confirm by expanding
steps.content: Expand the left side: $$3(kx+13)=3kx+39.$$ The equation is $$3kx+39=\frac{48}{17}x+36.$$ For no solution, match the $x$-coefficients: $$3k=\frac{48}{17}$$ $$k=\frac{48}{51}=\frac{16}{17}\approx0.9411.$$

steps.title: Check the contradiction
steps.content: With $k=\frac{16}{17}$, the $x$-terms match, but the constants are $39$ and $36$. After subtracting the matching $x$-terms, the equation becomes $39=36$, which is impossible. Therefore the equation has no solution, and the answer is .9411.
